what: Fitting and summarizing a basic model
title: Analyzing univariate recognition data with bayesroc
text: tutorial demonstrates bayesroc package can used analyze recognition data simple cognitive experiment. , use data Experiment 1B Whitridge et al. (2024). dataset shipped package. first load package dataset: several variables may want include models build: conf contains confidence ratings 6-point scale, old indicates whether items targets lures, condition indicates item studied (.e., speaking aloud, singing , reading silently), scale_wf gives scaled SUBTLEX word frequency item (Brysbaert & New, 2009), participant words subject- item-level indicators. start basic equal variance model includes fixed effects condition random effects subjects items. first specify formula using brf() function, requires ratings, old/new indicator, predictors want include. primary formula constructed form rating | is_old ~ predictors applied dprime (mu cumulative families). model parameters require additional formulas: , remove intercept dprime interpretability. criteria depend lure distribution condition applies targets, specify intercepts criterion formula. can pass formula data broc() function construct model: Similar point, encoding_vars argument used indicate condition can apply targets, common design within cognitive studies. failed specify condition encoding variable, model estimate several meaningless parameters (e.g., dprime lures) also enter random effects contaminate covariance structure altering implied prior. Thus, encoding_vars always used manipulations apply targets. constructed model, can run MCMC sampling using fit_broc function. , retain default settings use NumPyro backend speed: summary fitted model object contains estimates, CIs, diagnostics parameters. Note thresholds, K-1 correlated random intercepts per grouping level. ensure monotonicity, thresholds constructed midpoint (.e., threshold 3 K = 6) log-scale offsets. continuous covariates specified, summary.broc_fit() reconstructs natural scale population-level thresholds. also want put threshold correlations interpretable scale, can pass additional argument summary call: can also pass fitted model useful functions, plot_roc_curve() pp_check.broc_fit():

text: Formulas provided univariate bayesroc models must supply response variable binary indicator variable using either 0/1 -0.5/0.5 coding denotes whether trial lure target. variables provided form response | is_old ~ predictors, formula implictly applies d’ subsequent formulae provided model parameters (e.g., criterion ~ predictors, sigma ~ predictors). code shows hierarchical unequal variance signal detection model (UVSD) can fit bundled prm09 dataset (97 subjects, 480 items, 6-point confidence; Pratte et al., 2010), correlated subject- item-level random intercepts every parameter cross-correlated d’ sigma. view model summary plot model-implied ROC curve. dataset large, fit basic example first 20 participants. , resp rating response 6-point scale (higher responses indicating “sure old”) cond 0/1 coded indicator variable. interested modeling trial-level data (e.g., random effects items), can aggregate data per-subject counts fit quick version model. code: library(bayesroc) data(prm09)  first20 <- prm09[prm09$sub<20,]  m <- broc(brf(resp | cond ~ 1 + (1|s|sub) + (1|i|item),               criterion ~ 1 + (1|sub) + (1|item),               sigma ~ 1 + (1|s|sub) + (1|i|item),               family = uvsd()),           data = first20) fit <- fit_broc(m, backend = "stan")  summary(fit) plot_roc_curve(fit) agg <- as.data.frame(table(first20[c("sub", "cond", "resp")]),                       responseName = "count")  m <- broc(brf(resp | cond ~ 1 + (1|s|sub),               criterion ~ 1 + (1|sub),               sigma ~ 1 + (1|s|sub),               family = uvsd()),               counts = "count",           data = agg) fit <- fit_broc(m, backend = "stan")

title: Bayesian ROC-Based Signal Detection Theory Models
text: Stan backend uses fused likelihood analytic gradients default (batch_likelihood = TRUE), much faster plain Stan autodiff needs extra toolchain beyond cmdstanr. Pass batch_likelihood = FALSE force plain path (e.g. cross-checking). Stan backend single-threaded default supports multithreading via reduce_sum passing threads = TRUE broc() specifying threads_per_chain = N_threads calling fit_broc(). NumPyro backend (fit_broc(m, backend = "jax")) generally faster Stan uses analytic custom JVPs. backend supports multithreading number threads spawned chain depends model family, fixed- random- effects structure, dataset size. Threads per chain set directly. present, NumPyro backend CPU-, GPU support may added future releases.

title: Bivariate Cumulative Ordinal Model — bivariate_cumulative
text: Two correlated ordinal responses modelled jointly via bivariate normal CDF, SDT structure (Agresti, 2010).

title: Bivariate Cumulative Ordinal Model — bivariate_cumulative
code: bivariate_cumulative()

title: Bivariate Cumulative Ordinal Model — bivariate_cumulative
text: broc_family object.

title: Bivariate Cumulative Ordinal Model — bivariate_cumulative
text: two ordinal responses (Y_1, Y_2) cutpoints c1_1 < ... < c1_K_1-1 c2_1 < ... < c2_K_2-1, joint cumulative bivariate normal CDF: $$P(Y_1 \le j,\; Y_2 \le k \mid x) = \Phi_2\big(c1_j - \mu_1(x),\;       c2_k - \mu_2(x);\; \rho\big)$$ \(c_0 = -\infty\), \(c_K = +\infty\). Variance fixed 1 dimensions; \(\mu_1\) \(\mu_2\) intercept (absorbed cutpoints) identifiability. Cell probabilities 4-corner differencing: $$P(Y_1 = j,\; Y_2 = k \mid x) = P(\le j, \le k) - P(\le j-1, \le k)       - P(\le j, \le k-1) + P(\le j-1, \le k-1)$$

title: Bivariate Cumulative Ordinal Model — bivariate_cumulative
text: mu1 (required) Linear predictor dimension 1; intercept absorbed cutpoints. Identity link. mu2 (required) Linear predictor dimension 2. Identity link. cutpoints1 (required) K1-1 ordered cutpoints dimension 1 (mid-anchor + ordered gaps). Identity link. Set priors via thresh_mid log_gaps. cutpoints2 (required) K2-1 ordered cutpoints dimension 2. Identity link. Set priors via thresh_mid2 log_gaps2. rho (required) Correlation dimensions; effective \(\rho = \tanh(\rho_{\text{param}})\). Fisher z link.

title: Bivariate Cumulative Ordinal Model — bivariate_cumulative
code: broc(brf(resp(y1, y2) ~ x1,          mu2 ~ x2,          cutpoints1 ~ 1,          cutpoints2 ~ 1,          rho ~ 1,          family = bivariate_cumulative()),     data = dat)

title: Bivariate Dual Process Signal Detection Model — bivariate_dp
text: varying_source_criteria TRUE, estimate separate source criteria detection confidence level. bounded TRUE, fit BBDP variant (conditional source mean clamped new-item baseline). FALSE (default), fit BDP variant standard bivariate Gaussian familiarity. new_source_criteria varying_source_criteria = TRUE, source criteria structured across detection responses (ignored otherwise). "full" (default): every detection response level source criteria (K1 x (K2-1) total). "shared": responses judge item "new" share single set source criteria; requires old_levels. old_levels Integer vector detection response levels (1..K1 scale, e.g. old_levels = 4:6) count "old" judgements; rest "new". Required new_source_criteria = "shared"; ignored otherwise. varying_re Random-effect structure varying source criteria (distinct new_source_criteria, governs fixed criteria). "shared" (default): one source-criterion RE shift shared across detection bins. "per_bin": separate RE shift per detection bin. "full": separate RE every source threshold within every detection bin. Requires varying_source_criteria = TRUE unless "shared".

title: Bivariate Dual Process Signal Detection Model — bivariate_dp
text: old item, cell probability 3-component mixture recollection states: $$P(Y_1 = j,\; Y_2 = k \mid X) = (1 - \lambda)\,P_{\mathrm{F}}(j, k \mid X)       + \lambda(1 - \lambda_2)\,\mathbf{1}[j = K_1]\,P_{\mathrm{S}}(k \mid X)       + \lambda \lambda_2\,\mathbf{1}[j = K_1, k = k_X^*]$$ : lambda item-recollection probability, lambda_2 source-recollection probability (lambda, lambda2). \(P_{\mathrm{F}}(j, k \mid X)\) bivariate-Gaussian familiarity cell probability, defined cumulatively \(P_{\mathrm{F}}(Y_1 \le j, Y_2 \le k \mid X) = \Phi_2\big((c1_j - \mu_1)/\sigma_1,\;   (c2_k - \mu_2)/\sigma_2;\; \rho\big)\) 4-corner differencing, taking per-source \((\mu_1, \mu_2, \sigma_1, \sigma_2, \rho)\) bivariate_gaussian(). \(P_{\mathrm{S}}(k \mid X)\) marginal source cell probability (familiarity integrated ): \(P_{\mathrm{S}}(Y_2 \le k \mid X) =   \Phi((c2_k - \mu_2)/\sigma_2)\) 1D differencing. \(k_X^*\) recollection-success source response: \(k_X^* = 1\) X = , \(k_X^* = K_2\) X = B. signal correctly identified source recollection. new items: lambda = lambda_2 = 0, likelihood reduces \(P_{\mathrm{F}}\) alone new-item parameters. bounded (BBDP) variant, bivariate piece uses source-mean clamping Starns et al. (2014); otherwise (BDP) standard bivariate normal applies.

title: Bivariate Dual Process Signal Detection Model — bivariate_dp

Logit link. lambda_B (optional) Item-recollection probability Source B. omitted, constrained lambda_B = lambda. lambda2 (required) Source-recollection probability Source . Logit link. lambda2_B (optional) Source-recollection probability Source B. omitted, constrained lambda2_B = lambda2. Defaults bivariate parameters match bivariate_gaussian() – e.g. dprime_B defaults dprime, sigma defaults fixed 1, discrim_B defaults mirror-symmetric.

Default FALSE fits standard unbounded BG model (DeCarlo, 2003). new_source_criteria varying_source_criteria = TRUE, source criteria structured across detection responses (ignored otherwise). "full" (default): every detection response level source criteria (K1 x (K2-1) total). "shared": responses judge item "new" share single set source criteria; requires old_levels. old_levels Integer vector detection response levels (1..K1 scale, e.g. old_levels = 4:6) count "old" judgements; rest "new". Required new_source_criteria = "shared"; ignored otherwise. varying_re Random-effect structure varying source criteria (distinct new_source_criteria, governs fixed criteria). "shared" (default): one source-criterion RE shift shared across detection bins. "per_bin": separate RE shift per detection bin. "full": separate RE every source threshold within every detection bin. Requires varying_source_criteria = TRUE unless "shared".

title: Bivariate Gaussian Signal Detection Model — bivariate_gaussian
text: item type \(X \\{N, , B\}\), joint cumulative (detection, source) ratings bivariate normal CDF: $$P(Y_1 \le j,\; Y_2 \le k \mid X) = \Phi_2\!\Big(\tfrac{c1_j - \mu_1(X)}{\sigma_1(X)},\;       \tfrac{c2_k - \mu_2(X)}{\sigma_2(X)};\; \rho(X)\Big)$$ per-source means/SDs/correlation : X = N: \(\mu_1 = 0,\; \mu_2 = 0,\; \sigma_1 = \sigma_2 = 1,\; \rho = \rho_N\) X = : \(\mu_1 = d'_A,\; \mu_2 = \psi_A,\; \sigma_1 = \sigma_{1A},\; \sigma_2 = \sigma_{2A},\; \rho = \rho_A\) X = B: \(\mu_1 = d'_B,\; \mu_2 = \psi_B,\; \sigma_1 = \sigma_{1B},\; \sigma_2 = \sigma_{2B},\; \rho = \rho_B\) \(d'_A\) = dprime, \(d'_B\) = dprime_B, \(\psi_A\) = discrim, \(\psi_B\) = discrim_B. Cell probabilities 4-corner differencing: $$P(Y_1 = j,\; Y_2 = k \mid X) = P(\le j, \le k) - P(\le j-1, \le k)       - P(\le j, \le k-1) + P(\le j-1, \le k-1)$$ bounded mode, conditional source mean clamped 0 per-source crossover point, source items never -chance source discrimination low item evidence (Starns et al., 2014).

title: Bivariate Gaussian Signal Detection Model — bivariate_gaussian
text: dprime (required) \(d'_A\) detection axis. Identity link (unbounded) log link (bounded). discrim (required) Signed source-axis position items. Identity link (unbounded) log link (bounded – positive magnitude, placed negative source axis via internal negation). criterion (required) K1-1 detection thresholds. Identity link. criterion2 (required) K2-1 source thresholds. Identity link. rho (required) Detection-source correlation items. Fisher z link (unbounded) logistic (bounded). dprime_B (optional) \(d'_B\) B items. omitted, constrained dprime_B = dprime (equal detection sensitivity). discrim_B (optional) Signed source-axis position B items. omitted, constrained mirror-symmetric (unbounded: discrim_B = -discrim; bounded: magnitude discrim, placed positive source axis). sigma (optional) SD detection axis items. omitted, fixed 1. Log link. sigma_B (optional) SD detection axis B items. omitted, constrained sigma_B = sigma. sigma2 (optional) SD source axis items. omitted, fixed 1. Log link. sigma2_B (optional) SD source axis B items. omitted, constrained sigma2_B = sigma2. rho_B (optional) Detection-source correlation B items. omitted, constrained mirror-symmetric rho. rho_N (optional) Detection-source correlation new items. Fisher z link. omitted, fixed 0.

title: Compose a Multi-Parameter SDT Model Formula — brf
text: formula Two-sided formula primary parameter (d' / mu), response | is_old ~ predictors. is_old may coded 0/1 (new/old) centered (-0.5/0.5); centered coding supported lure mixtures multi-response families. multi-response families, use resp() left-hand side. ... Additional one-sided formulas, one per parameter, parameter name left. E.g. criterion ~ 1 + (1 | subj), sigma ~ cond, lambda ~ 1. family SDT model family: one evsd(), uvsd(), dpsd(), mixture(), source_mixture(), bivariate_gaussian(), bivariate_dp(), vrdp2d(), cdp(), cumulative(), bivariate_cumulative(). May also set brf(); value broc() takes precedence. encoding_vars Character vector column names treat encoding-phase (study) manipulations lure items meaningful value. Listing column auto-wraps encoding(). value passed broc() takes precedence one set . counts Name column giving per-row trial counts, aggregated (count) data rather one row per trial. May also set brf(). cor_threshold TRUE (default), random effects K-1 thresholds modeled jointly (correlated) via threshold parameterization; FALSE treats independent. threads TRUE, enable within-chain reduce_sum threading Stan backend (set thread count per chain via fit_broc(threads_per_chain=)). Default FALSE. gap_link Link threshold gaps: "log" (default) "softplus". NULL uses family default.

May also set brf(). cor_threshold TRUE (default), random effects K-1 thresholds modeled jointly (correlated) via threshold parameterization; FALSE treats independent. threads TRUE, enable within-chain reduce_sum threading Stan backend (set thread count per chain via fit_broc(threads_per_chain=)). title: Set a Prior on an SDT Model Parameter — broc_prior

title: Set a Prior on an SDT Model Parameter — broc_prior
code: broc_prior(prior, class, dpar = NULL, coef = NULL, group = NULL)

title: Set a Prior on an SDT Model Parameter — broc_prior
text: prior prior distribution call, e.g. "normal(0, 1)", "student_t(3, 0, 2.5)", "lkj(2)". Supported backends: normal, std_normal, student_t, cauchy, logistic, gumbel, double_exponential (= laplace), uniform, beta, exponential, gamma, inv_gamma, weibull, lognormal, half forms half_normal, half_cauchy, half_student_t, lkj (class = "cor" ). SD priors (class = "sd"/"sds") symmetric distribution automatically restricted positive values. Stan backend additionally accepts distribution Stan language. class Parameter class: "b" (fixed effects), "sd" (random-effect SDs), "cor" (random-effect correlations), "sds" (smooth penalty SDs, models s()/t2() smooth terms). dpar Distributional parameter prior applies , e.g. "dprime", "sigma", "lambda". criterion priors use "thresh_mid" / "log_gaps" ("thresh_mid2" / "log_gaps2" second response axis bivariate CDP families). Family-specific aliases (mu, mu1, mu2, rec, fam, sigma_R, sigma_F) also accepted. title: Set a Prior on an SDT Model Parameter — broc_prior
text: Priors matched least specific: coef-level prior overrides group-level one, overrides dpar-level one, overrides global class default. Call get_broc_prior() broc() brf() object (data) see coefficients exist current prior . Confidence thresholds set directly. reparameterized central anchor (thresh_mid) plus strictly-positive, log-scale spacings (log_gaps) successive thresholds, enforces ordering. title: Continuous Dual Process Signal Detection Model — cdp
text: Recollection R familiarity F independent Gaussians: \(R \sim \mathcal{N}(\mu_R, \sigma_R)\), \(F \sim \mathcal{N}(\mu_F, \sigma_F)\) targets, \((\mu_R, \mu_F) = (0, 0)\) \((\sigma_R, \sigma_F) = (1, 1)\) lures. F (familiarity) dimension cumulative : $$P(Y \le k \mid \mathrm{old} = 0) = \Phi(c_k), \quad       P(Y \le k \mid \mathrm{old} = 1) = \Phi\!\Big(\tfrac{c_k - \mu_F}{\sigma_F}\Big)$$ ratings k old_levels (new-side responses), F cell (differencing) full likelihood. ratings k old_levels (old-side responses), cell factors across F (bin) R (Remember vs Know): $$P(Y = k,\; \mathrm{rk} \mid \mathrm{old}) =       \big[P(Y \le k) - P(Y \le k - 1)\big] \cdot P(\mathrm{rk} \mid \mathrm{old})$$ rk factor cumulative R dimension. targets: $$P(\mathrm{rk} = K \mid \mathrm{old} = 1) = \Phi\!\Big(\tfrac{c_R - \mu_R}{\sigma_R}\Big), \quad       P(\mathrm{rk} = R \mid \mathrm{old} = 1) = 1 - \Phi\!\Big(\tfrac{c_R - \mu_R}{\sigma_R}\Big)$$ (lures use form \(\mu_R = 0, \sigma_R = 1\)). 3-level R/K/G Know criterion \(c_K\), K row becomes \(\Phi((c_R - \mu_R)/\sigma_R) - \Phi((c_K - \mu_R)/\sigma_R)\) \(P(\mathrm{rk} = G) = \Phi((c_K - \mu_R)/\sigma_R)\).

title: Continuous Dual Process Signal Detection Model — cdp
text: rec (required) Recollection mean \(\mu_R\) targets. Identity link. fam (required) Familiarity mean \(\mu_F\) targets. Identity link. criterion (required) Confidence thresholds F dimension. Identity link. rec_crit (required) Recollection criterion \(c_R\) separating Remember vs Know responses R dimension. Identity link. sigma_R (optional) SD recollection targets. omitted, fixed 1. Log link. sigma_F (optional) SD familiarity targets. omitted, fixed 1. Log link. know_crit (optional) Know criterion \(c_K\) R dimension 3-level R/K/G case (rk values 1=R, 2=K, 3=G). Identity link. omitted, R/K modeled.

text: rk values: 1 = Remember, 2 = Know, (3 = Guess R/K/G mode).

text: new items, responses follow standard EVSD (recollection): $$P(Y \le k \mid \mathrm{old} = 0) = \Phi(c_k)$$ old items, recollection succeeds probability lambda, producing highest-confidence "old" response Y = K. Otherwise, familiarity-distribution UVSD mean d' SD sigma: $$P(Y \le k \mid \mathrm{old} = 1) = (1 - \lambda)\,\Phi\!\Big(\frac{c_k - d'}{\sigma}\Big)       \quad (k < K)$$ $$P(Y \le K \mid \mathrm{old} = 1) = 1$$

Cell probabilities differencing: \(P(Y = k \mid \mathrm{old}) = P(Y \le k \mid \mathrm{old}) - P(Y \le k - 1 \mid \mathrm{old})\) \(P(Y \le 0) = 0\) \(P(Y \le K) = 1\). new- old-item distributions unit SD.

text: Mixture two latent classes (DeCarlo, 2002), expressed cumulatively: $$P(Y \le k \mid \mathrm{old}) = \lambda\,\Phi\!\Big(\tfrac{c_k - d'_1 \cdot \mathrm{old}}{\sigma_1}\Big)       + (1 - \lambda)\,\Phi\!\Big(\tfrac{c_k - d'_2 \cdot \mathrm{old}}{\sigma_2}\Big)$$ lambda mixing weight state 1 (lambda), \((d'_1, \sigma_1)\) = (dprime, sigma), \((d'_2, \sigma_2)\) = (dprime2, sigma2). old = 0 Gaussians collapse noise N(0, 1) baseline, new items reduce standard EVSD. lure mixture specified (dprime_L lambda_L), new items get mixture: $$P(Y \le k \mid \mathrm{old} = 0) = (1 - \lambda_L)\,\Phi(c_k)       + \lambda_L\,\Phi\!\Big(\tfrac{c_k - d'_L}{\sigma_L}\Big)$$ dprime dprime2 free, ordered constraint (dprime > dprime2) imposed identifiability.

title: Mixture Signal Detection Model — mixture
text: _L suffix marks optional lure-item mixture component (applied new items); unsuffixed parameters describe old-item mixture. dprime (required) \(d'_1\) high (state 1) old-item component. Identity link. criterion (required) K-1 thresholds. Identity link. lambda (required) Mixing weight state 1. Logit link. dprime2 (optional) \(d'_2\) state 2. omitted, fixed 0. Identity link. sigma (optional) SD \(\sigma_1\) state 1. omitted, fixed 1. Log link. sigma2 (optional) SD \(\sigma_2\) state 2. omitted, fixed 1. Log link. dprime_L (optional) d' additional lure-item mixture component (new items ). Identity link. omitted, lure mixture (new items standard EVSD N(0, 1)). lambda_L (optional) Mixing weight lure-mixture component (new items ). Logit link. Required (dprime_L) enable lure mixture. sigma_L (optional) SD lure-mixture component. omitted, fixed 1. Log link.

title: Source Mixture Signal Detection Model — source_mixture
text: Three latent Gaussian components, unit variance: nonattended 0 (shared), attended Source \(d'_A\) (typically negative), attended Source B \(d'_B\) (typically positive). source, source-rating cumulative mixture attended nonattended: $$P(Y \le k \mid \mathrm{source} = ) = \lambda_A\,\Phi(c_k - d'_A)       + (1 - \lambda_A)\,\Phi(c_k)$$ $$P(Y \le k \mid \mathrm{source} = B) = \lambda_B\,\Phi(c_k - d'_B)       + (1 - \lambda_B)\,\Phi(c_k)$$ lambda_A, lambda_B source-conditional attention probabilities (lambda, lambda_B). overall source discriminability \(d_{AB} = d'_B - d'_A\).

title: Source Mixture Signal Detection Model — source_mixture
text: dprime (required) \(d'_A\): location attended Source distribution. Identity link. criterion (required) K-1 ordered confidence thresholds (mid-anchor + ordered gaps). Identity link. lambda (required) Attention probability Source . Logit link. dprime_B (optional) \(d'_B\): location attended Source B distribution. Identity link. omitted, constrained dprime_B = -dprime. lambda_B (optional) Attention probability Source B. Logit link. omitted, constrained lambda_B = lambda.

title: Source Mixture Signal Detection Model — source_mixture
text: conditioning variable | source variable (2-level factor 0/1 indicator Source vs Source B).

text: Noise distribution N(0, 1); signal distribution N(d', sigma). old/new judgment Y K-point scale (old 0, 1) criteria c_1 < ... < c_K-1: $$P(Y \le k \mid \mathrm{old} = 0) = \Phi(c_k)$$ $$P(Y \le k \mid \mathrm{old} = 1) = \Phi\!\Big(\frac{c_k - d'}{\sigma}\Big)$$ k = 1, ..., K-1.

title: Two-Dimensional Variable Recollection Dual Process Model — vrdp2d
text: varying_source_criteria TRUE, estimate separate source criteria item confidence level (K1 x (K2-1) source thresholds); full model Onyper et al. (2010). FALSE (default), single set source criteria (K2-1 thresholds) shared across detection levels. varying_re Random-effect structure varying source criteria. "shared" (default): one source-criterion RE shift shared across detection bins. "per_bin": separate RE shift per detection bin. "full": separate RE every source threshold within every detection bin. Requires varying_source_criteria = TRUE unless "shared".

title: Two-Dimensional Variable Recollection Dual Process Model — vrdp2d
text: states diagonal covariance (zero detection-source correlation), joint cumulative factors across dimensions. new items: $$P(Y_1 \le j,\; Y_2 \le k \mid X = N) = \Phi(c1_j)\,\Phi(c2_k)$$ old items, recollection succeeds probability lambda. cumulative 2-component mixture recollection: $$P(Y_1 \le j,\; Y_2 \le k \mid X = \mathrm{old}) =       (1 - \lambda)\,\Phi\!\Big(\tfrac{c1_j - d'_F}{\sigma_1}\Big)\Phi(c2_k)       + \lambda\,\Phi\!\Big(\tfrac{c1_j - d'_F - d'_R}{\sigma_1}\Big)         \Phi\!\Big(\tfrac{c2_k - \psi_X}{\sigma_2}\Big)$$ \(\psi_A = +d'_S, \psi_B = -d'_S\) (recollection-conditional source location vs B), lambda recollection probability (lambda). non-recollected component zero source mean (source info); recollected component carries source discriminability.

title: Two-Dimensional Variable Recollection Dual Process Model — vrdp2d
text: dprime (required) \(d'_F\): familiarity strength item dimension. Identity link. dprime2 (required) \(d'_R\): recollection boost item dimension. Identity link. discrim (required) \(d'_S\): source discriminability recollected items (non-recollected items center 0 source). Identity link. lambda (required) Recollection probability. Logit link. criterion (required) K1-1 item-dimension thresholds. Identity link. criterion2 (required) K2-1 source-dimension thresholds. Identity link. sigma (optional) SD item dimension. omitted, fixed 1. Log link. sigma2 (optional) SD source dimension recollected items. omitted, fixed 1. Log link. discrim_B (optional) Source discriminability B items. omitted, constrained mirror : discrim_B = -discrim.
